question: Which projects or companies issue stablecoins on Stellar?

golden.answer:
A grounded answer derives its named examples and status claims from the live Stellar Scout directory or research data, then dates the roster so it is not presented as permanent. As of 2026-07-11, a live stablecoin-issuer search returned 38 matches, with Circle, Glo Dollar, and Coins.ph among the first results.

The dated example is illustrative: a later grounded answer should report what the same source basis supports at its own observation time while preserving the stable distinctions above.
keyFacts: Presents specific source-supported content as a current or dated observation rather than as an unsupported memory claim.
Makes the as-of date visible for every changeable roster, status, version, or measurement.
Names Circle (USDC and EURC) as the dominant stablecoin issuer on Stellar.
avoid: Do NOT fabricate stablecoin issuers or invent token tickers not present in the corpus.
Do NOT attribute USDC/EURC to a non-Circle issuer.
Do NOT present the observed live result set as a complete or permanent roster; explicitly historical facts remain historical.
golden.notes:
Discovery → scout_projects. Freshness-sensitive (issuer set evolves). Dossier §4.4. The dominant, verifiable anchor is Circle (USDC + EURC); the broader issuer set must be grounded in the live directory, not invented. Verified 2026-06-29: stellar.org/products-and-tools/circle-usdc-eurc resolves and confirms Circle issues both USDC and EURC on Stellar.
Also good if the answer: Surfaces additional anchor-operated / fiat-pegged issuers from the directory rather than only naming Circle. | Frames the answer as a discovery over the project directory, attributing issuers to specific entities.
Golden cites: The Stellar Light / Scout project directory or a stellar.org stablecoin page.

verified.by: P4 conversions rework after independent Fable copy/quality review
evidence: 2026-07-11 localhost:8787 search→execute probe observed: a live stablecoin-issuer search returned 38 matches, with Circle, Glo Dollar, and Coins.ph among the first results.
